question_title: Will Apple explicitly mention or display "Google" or "Gemini" during the WWDC 2026 keynote on June 8, 2026?
summary: Apple will very likely mention or display "Google" or "Gemini" during the WWDC 2026 keynote. The strongest evidence is the combination of (1) a 100% base rate of Apple naming AI partners at recent WWDCs (ChatGPT at 2024 and 2025), (2) iOS 27's Extensions feature letting users choose between ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini — which would display these names on-screen during demos, and (3) the low resolution bar requiring only spoken OR on-screen text. The main risk for NO is Apple's deliberate control of pre-recorded keynotes, their reported reluctance to highlight Google due to privacy branding, and the possibility of demoing Extensions generically without naming specific providers. However, the precedent of naming AI partners and the Extensions UI make it very difficult for Apple to avoid "Gemini" appearing somewhere in a 2-hour keynote. Factor correlation (all YES factors fail simultaneously if Apple deliberately avoids naming) justifies discounting from the ~95% factor-implied probability down to 80%.

factors.description: Strong precedent: Apple mentioned 'ChatGPT' and 'OpenAI' on stage at both WWDC 2024 and 2025 when those were the AI partners, establishing a clear pattern of naming AI vendor partners during keynotes.

iOS 27 Extensions system: Bloomberg and MacRumors report iOS 27 will let users choose between ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini. Demonstrating this feature at the keynote would almost certainly show these brand names on-screen in the UI.

Low resolution bar: Question resolves YES if EITHER spoken OR shown as on-screen text. On-screen text in UI mockups/demos is much harder to avoid during a 2-hour keynote demonstrating iOS 27 features.
Apple reportedly reluctant to highlight Google's role due to privacy branding concerns. Google is associated with advertising, clashing with Apple's privacy positioning.

Apple emphasizing on-device AI at WWDC 2026 per The Information/MacRumors reporting, framing local processing as competitive advantage. Naming Google/Gemini as cloud backbone undermines this narrative.
'Gemini' vs 'Google' distinction: Apple may avoid 'Google' (company) but could still show/say 'Gemini' (model name). Either word resolves YES. 'Gemini' carries less advertising baggage.
WWDC 2025: Apple did NOT mention 'Google' in spoken keynote narration. Shows willingness to omit Google from keynotes. However, Google was not an AI partner at that time.

Extensions feature too major to skip at keynote: Bloomberg/MacRumors report it as a flagship iOS 27 feature. Apple consistently announces major OS features at the keynote, and Extensions with named AI providers would naturally be shown.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 80% YES** — Apple will likely mention or display "Google" or "Gemini" during the WWDC 2026 keynote.

**Key reasoning:**

1. **Strong precedent (100% base rate):** Apple named ChatGPT and OpenAI on stage at both WWDC 2024 and 2025, establishing a clear pattern of crediting AI partners during keynotes.

2. **iOS 27 Extensions feature is the strongest mechanism:** Bloomberg and MacRumors confirm iOS 27 will let users choose between ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini for Apple Intelligence features. Demonstrating this flagship feature would almost certainly show "Gemini" on screen in the settings UI.

3. **Low resolution bar:** The question resolves YES if either spoken aloud OR shown as on-screen text — making it very hard to avoid during a 2-hour keynote demoing iOS 27 features that include named AI provider choices.

4. **Main risk for NO (~20%):** Apple has absolute control of the pre-recorded keynote and is reportedly reluctant to highlight Google. They could demo Extensions generically, save the detailed demo for the Platforms State of the Union (which doesn't count), or frame everything under "Apple Foundation Models" without crediting Google/Gemini. Apple's emphasis on on-device AI at this WWDC could also lead to downplaying cloud partnerships.

resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if either the word "Google" or the word "Gemini" (case-insensitive, including as part of brand phrases such as "Google Cloud" or "Gemini models") is spoken aloud by an Apple presenter, or appears as visible on-screen text in a slide, demo, or graphic, at any point during the official WWDC 2026 opening Keynote presentation streamed on June 8, 2026 from Apple Park. The official keynote video will be posted to https://www.apple.com/apple-events/ and to Apple's YouTube channel shortly after the event; resolution will be based on reviewing that video and any associated captions/transcript. Otherwise the question resolves NO. Mentions in the separately-broadcast "Platforms State of the Union" session, in developer sessions, in press releases, in newsroom articles, or in any post-keynote interviews do NOT count — only the opening Keynote itself counts. If the keynote is postponed past June 12, 2026, the question resolves NO. If the keynote video is not publicly available by June 14, 2026, resolution may use reputable contemporaneous coverage of the keynote from [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions).
fine_print: Incidental appearances of the words in URLs at the bottom of legal/disclaimer slides, or in third-party logos shown only as part of a generic device home-screen mockup (e.g., a Gmail icon in a montage of installed apps), do not count. A clear reference to "Google" the company, a Google product (e.g., Google Search, Google Cloud), or "Gemini" the AI model — whether spoken or shown as on-screen text in a feature slide — does count.

factors.description: Strong precedent: Apple mentioned 'ChatGPT' and 'OpenAI' on stage at both WWDC 2024 and 2025 when those were the AI partners. This establishes a clear pattern of naming AI vendor partners during keynotes.

iOS 27 Extensions system: Bloomberg and MacRumors report iOS 27 will let users choose between ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini for Apple Intelligence features. Demonstrating this feature at the keynote would almost certainly show these brand names on-screen in the UI, even if presenters avoid saying them aloud.

Low resolution bar: Question resolves YES if EITHER spoken OR shown as on-screen text. On-screen text is much harder to avoid when demoing a feature that displays AI provider choices. Even small text in a settings screen mockup would count.
Apple reportedly reluctant to highlight Google's role due to privacy branding concerns. Google is associated with advertising, clashing with Apple's privacy positioning.
Apple emphasizing on-device AI at WWDC 2026, framing local processing as competitive advantage rather than highlighting cloud partnerships. Could lead to downplaying Gemini's role.

'Gemini' vs 'Google' branding distinction: Apple may avoid saying 'Google' (the company) but could still show/say 'Gemini' (the model name). Either word resolves YES. 'Gemini' carries less advertising baggage than 'Google'.
WWDC 2025 precedent: Apple did NOT mention 'Google' in spoken keynote narration even though Google was referenced in the press release. Shows Apple's willingness to omit Google from keynotes.

Extensions feature is too major to skip at keynote: This is a flagship iOS 27 feature per Bloomberg/MacRumors reporting. Apple always announces major OS features at the keynote, and Extensions with named AI providers would be shown.

input.anchor: Base rate from precedent: Apple mentioned its AI partner (ChatGPT/OpenAI) on stage at 2/2 recent WWDCs (2024, 2025) = 100%. But this is a different partner (Google) with known branding tensions, so discount to ~70-75%.
input.assessment:
The question asks whether Apple will say or show 'Google' or 'Gemini' at any point during the WWDC 2026 keynote on June 8, 2026.

**FOR YES (~80%):**
The strongest evidence is the combination of: (1) a 100% base rate of Apple naming AI partners on stage at recent WWDCs, (2) iOS 27's Extensions system that explicitly lets users choose between ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini - which would display these names in the UI during any demo, and (3) the low resolution threshold requiring only spoken OR on-screen text appearance.

Apple's $1B/year Gemini deal was publicly announced in a joint statement. There's no secrecy. The Extensions feature is reported as a major iOS 27 feature that Apple would demo at the keynote. When Apple showed the AI engine selection at WWDC 2024 (On-Device/Cloud/ChatGPT), they explicitly showed 'ChatGPT' on screen. The same logic applies to showing 'Gemini' in the Extensions UI.

**FOR NO (~20%):**
Apple is reportedly reluctant to highlight Google because of Google's advertising association. Apple emphasized on-device AI as a key WWDC focus. Apple could potentially demo Extensions without naming specific providers, or save the detailed Extensions demo for the Platforms State of the Union (which doesn't count). At WWDC 2025, Apple avoided saying 'Google' in the keynote. Apple could rebrand everything under 'Apple Intelligence' or 'Apple Foundation Models'.

**Key tension:** The question doesn't require Apple to EMPHASIZE Google/Gemini - just to mention or display it once. This low bar, combined with the Extensions UI that would show brand names, makes it very difficult for Apple to avoid entirely during a 2-hour keynote demonstrating iOS 27 features.
input.calibration_notes: My anchor is ~75% based on the precedent of naming AI partners, discounted from 100% for the Google-specific branding tension. The factors push this up to ~80%, driven primarily by the Extensions feature and low resolution bar. I'm not near 50% so no hedging concern. The main risk of overconfidence is if Apple finds a way to demo Extensions without naming providers, or saves it for non-keynote sessions.
input.key_uncertainties: 1. Will Apple demo the Extensions feature at the keynote vs saving it for developer sessions? 2. Could Apple demo Extensions with generic placeholders instead of real provider names? 3. How strictly will Apple control what appears on-screen during demos (e.g., a Siri interaction that shows 'powered by Gemini' in small text)?
input.tool_audit: research() returned comprehensive findings from 3 parallel queries covering the partnership details, branding analysis, and WWDC history. web_search provided current May-June 2026 reporting. fetch_url successfully extracted information from MacRumors, Newsweek, felloai, marketingcode, implicator, and Engadget. Bloomberg was paywalled (403). get_coherence_links returned empty. get_metaculus_questions confirmed no community prediction available (AIB tournament).
input.update_triggers: 1. Leaked keynote rehearsal showing/not showing Gemini branding. 2. Apple pre-event press materials that name/avoid naming Google or Gemini. 3. Reports from reliable sources (Gurman, etc.) about Apple's specific branding plans for the keynote. 4. News that the Extensions feature has been delayed past WWDC.
input.process_reflection: The research tools worked well for this question. The research() sub-agent provided thorough historical context. Web search surfaced recent May 2026 articles that were crucial for understanding the Extensions feature and on-device AI emphasis. The main friction was Bloomberg's paywall, but enough secondary coverage existed. The question is well-structured with clear resolution criteria. No subforecast was needed since this is a direct binary without decomposable components.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
## Verification Summary

### Resolution Criteria
The agent fetched the full Metaculus question page (post_id 43742) and has the complete resolution criteria. The factors and assessment correctly engage with the specific terms: spoken OR on-screen text, only the opening Keynote counts (not Platforms State of the Union), and the fine print about incidental appearances in URLs or generic home-screen mockups not counting.

### Factor-by-Factor Verification

**Factor 1 (ChatGPT precedent, +1.5):** The trace confirms this via the question's background_info ("at WWDC 2024 and WWDC 2025 Apple repeatedly mentioned 'ChatGPT' by name during the keynote") and the agent's independent research on Wikipedia/WWDC history (line 224: "Apple expanded ChatGPT integrations into Visual Intelligence, Shortcuts..."). Minor note: The factor claims "OpenAI" was mentioned at both WWDCs — the WWDC 2024 OpenAI mention is well-confirmed, but WWDC 2025 "OpenAI" specifically isn't independently verified in the trace (only "ChatGPT" is). This is a trivial detail that doesn't affect the core conclusion that Apple names AI partners on stage.

**Factor 2 (iOS 27 Extensions, +1.5):** Well-grounded. The MacRumors article (fetched at line 593-608) confirms Bloomberg reports iOS 27 Extensions letting users choose between ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini. Multiple independent sources corroborate (AI2Work, TrendingTopics, tbreak, Yaabot).

**Factor 3 (low resolution bar, +0.8):** Correctly derived from the resolution criteria — "spoken aloud by an Apple presenter, or appears as visible on-screen text." This is a valid structural observation.

**Factor 4 (Apple reluctance, -0.8):** Grounded in the question's background_info and the agent's research (line 140: "Apple's own language: Consistently uses 'Apple Foundation Models' and 'Apple Intelligence'").

**Factor 5 (on-device AI emphasis, -0.5):** Grounded in the MacRumors article from May 28 (fetched at line 570-587), which reports Apple plans to "highlight its on-device AI capabilities as a competitive advantage."

**Factor 6 (Gemini vs Google branding, +0.5):** Valid reasoning — either word resolves YES per criteria. No specific trace source contradicts this.

**Factor 7 (WWDC 2025 no Google mention, -0.5):** Sourced from the question's background_info (line 41). The agent correctly notes in the thinking section (line 796) that at WWDC 2025, Google wasn't even an AI partner, which weakens this as a precedent. The confidence is appropriately low (0.6).

**Factor 8 (Extensions too major to skip, +0.8):** Grounded in the Bloomberg/MacRumors reporting about Extensions being a major iOS 27 feature.

### Double-Counting Check
Factors 1, 2, 3, and 8 are correlated — they all benefit from the scenario where Apple demos Extensions on stage. The agent explicitly acknowledges this correlation in the thinking section (line 812: "Several of my YES factors are overlapping... If Apple decides to avoid naming providers, ALL of these factors fail simultaneously"). The 80% probability (vs. the ~95% implied by the factor sum of ~3.0 logits) reflects this discount. This self-awareness is appropriate.

### Wrong-Direction Check
All factor signs are consistent with their descriptions. No issues.

### Contradictory Assessment Check
The narrative assessment reaches 80% YES, which is consistent with the factors collectively pointing toward YES with appropriate discount for correlation.

### Weak Counterargument Check
The counterargument is substantive. It identifies: (1) Apple's absolute control of pre-recorded keynotes, (2) the ChatGPT precedent being weaker than it appears because ChatGPT was the only option while Extensions involves multiple providers, (3) "Apple Foundation Models" branding existing to avoid crediting Google, (4) the Gemini foundation model role being different from ChatGPT's user-facing role. This is a genuine alternative story. Not a token gesture.

### Overconfident Self-Assessment Check
confidence_in_estimate = 0.50 — appropriately modest for a forward-looking question about a deliberate editorial decision by Apple, 7 days before the event.

### Probability Assessment
My independent estimate based on the evidence: ~75-80%. The 100% base rate for naming AI partners (ChatGPT at WWDC 2024/2025) is strong, but the discount for Apple's demonstrated reluctance with Google branding and control over pre-recorded content is legitimate. The Extensions UI showing brand names is the strongest mechanism for YES, but Apple could demo it generically. The low resolution bar (on-screen text counts) pushes it higher. 80% is at the upper end of my range but not meaningfully wrong — within 5pp of my central estimate.

### Anchor Divergence Check
Anchor: 70-75%. Final: 80%. The 5-10pp departure is justified by the Extensions feature evidence (new since the anchor was set) and the low resolution bar. No concern.

### Tool Audit Check
The agent reports 1 Bloomberg 403 error. Actual metrics: 8 fetch_url calls, 12.5% error rate = 1 error. Consistent. No discrepancy.

### Worldview Consistency Check
The worldview research files are the same three entries generated by this session's research queries. No contradictory prior research found. No numeric sub-forecasts on this question exist.

No errors found that would change the forecast direction or magnitude.
